message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
How many reports Bob and in what regions?
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Hmm? that sounds strange. @PLAYER can you take point and start sizing this up?
Can we get someone to reproduce on the site?
:star: Reproduces the incident
I can help you with the customer reports
Checking reports...
I checked the site. I was able to place an order
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
Okay can we get confirmation that it's just the emails that are displaying the incorrect items?
Gathering more information....
@daniel can you check the back end to see that the orders still contain the right items?
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
I found the issue. The names and their images are mismatched. Is it same for you @PLAYER ?
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
@shay Can you give me the latest changes shipped to the app?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
Sure i can check it. Before that i need one incident
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @PLAYER What shall we do now?
Is there a way we can pause sending the email confirmations out for a short time?
if the orders are indeed correct in the back end
I'll open up an incident
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
I heard there are few reports from customers

I can confirm that we have reports of an incident on the platform. It seems that customers are reporting the incorrect item being included in their email confirmations once they place an order. I'm confirming impact now
:star: Qualify the problem
@bob how many reports are there?
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
So far 40+ reports
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
@PLAYER Could you please discuss this with Bez?
I tried replicating this issue. Can see that images and names are mismatched.
@PLAYER let us know if you need any help from us
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
Before we cancel the orders, lets confirm impact
Take it forward with team @PLAYER , let me know if you face any roadblocks or need my help
What's the impact?
So far we've had So far 40+ reports
Customers are confused on what they will be getting in the delivery
:star: Incident record created
*Incident Number INC100078:* P2 email confirmations for orders are displaying the incorrect information
Incident has been opened
Few of them have placed by checking name and few of them placed order by checking image
How big is this problem?
@daniel can you check that the orders in the back end are actually being saved correctly?
Checking logs now in the backend
I can take a look into our services now
We don't have any alerts in the system
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Even i don't see any issue
I'd suggest lets put up a maintenance banner. By this we can control the damage to some extent
Must be network or platform issue.
Ask Daniel , he can assist you with putting up a banner on the site
Do we think we should pause the email confirmations being sent out so we can still receive orders? Or should we pause orders all together?
:star: Form a working theory
Its better we pause the orders until we have clarity on where things are going wrong
I can request a temporary banner
I can put this up
@PLAYER shall i ?
Thanks , go ahead

Okay, Daniel can you place a banner up and stop new orders from coming in? The banner should include some details that "we're having some issues with order confirmations, so we're temporarily pausing any new orders until the issue is resolved"
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
Daniel is placing a banner up now and disabling the ability to place new orders on the site until we get this resolved
Doing it now
We should be able to diagnose the issue and get a working theory to you in the next 5-10 minutes
:star: Track actions and time box them
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
Great , good decision
I'll confirm in here once we have the diagnosis and how long resolution will take
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
thanks!
@PLAYER How does it look now?
@PLAYER Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
No worries, focus on triaging. And update us when you have critical info
Looks like that's worked and the platform is in maintenance mode
We had two changes to the Cache and the Cart service recently
how do those two service interact with the email service? @tanya @daniel ?
This issue looks like a misconfiguration. We should pull in platform team on this
Checking now...
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug
But it's not a platform issue.
Cart redis pulls the data from catalogue service
For now lets try and post out data and information we have, lets limit pointing fingers at other teams
:star: Facts over opinion
My change is not a concern. It was just meant for UAT and its fine there
You're right @PLAYER , lets have Hamed's opinion as well
@hamed any thoughts here?
What have we found so far ?
No big signals at the moment in terms of the data that's been posted
:star: Use metrics as evidence
We have shipped three changes recently, one change to the front end service, one to the redis cache and one to the cart service
I think data has been misconfigured on prod. Check with team @PLAYER . I strongly suspect this
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
Are we able to roll back all three of those changes and verify that emails are working as expected in our testing environment?
@PLAYER if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
I doubt these changes are causing this issue
Where is this configuration handled or managed?
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @PLAYER
At what time Tanya?
Around 20 mins back
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Not right now Bez, the platform is currently down for maintenance any way
Ok here is what i found
I need to work on co-ordinating a resolution
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
It's important I focus there
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
Sure keep moving. Feel free to pull in if you need any help from us
We have some ideas about misconfigurations, confirming that now
Lets roll that change back
Right! let me re-verify my last change
Then we can run the same checks to see if those product id match again
How long is that going to take @daniel ?
:star: Manage actions and timings
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
It your call @PLAYER
Let's not take action just yet
Focus there. Update me once you have it

We don't know what other unintended consequences there might be for violating and changing the underlying database data @daniel. For now I think it best that we try and restore the database to the last known good state
do you have a rollback strategy for your script?
I think we should do Tanya's way. If the data source itself is wrong, then how would restarting help here
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
Tanya is right. We need to rehydrate the DB with correct mappings
I agree with Tanya
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@PLAYER please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @PLAYER?
Any blockers @PLAYER ?
How long will this restore take?
@tanya you can perform the first two steps now
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
2-3 mins
Step 1 is now complete
Moving on to step 2
Step 2 complete
No blockers at the moment. We've identified some misconfigured data in our database related to products. We're going to rehydrate the database to recover things back to normal.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
Can we verify that the product listings are correct?
This is great. Atleast you have got a working theory and steps to fix
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
On it
Until we've validated, we won't be able to bring the platform back up. Just trying to set expectations here that there might be other issues
I'll keep you updated
@PLAYER Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Is there a way for us to resend the confirmation emails once this is resolved?
Make sure we lift the maintenance banner after post-fix validations in backend
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
We can work on it. Right now banner is still there
Are there any other checks we can do to confirm problem is resolved?
We have just once data point at the moment, and I don't want to lift the banner until I know this is resolved for sure
We should be good to bring the site up again
I can confirm the mappings are fine now
Shall i lift the banner now ?
Lift the banner
Maintenance page removal in progress...
Issue seems to be resolved in the back end
Lifting banner now
We are LIVE for orders back again!
Lets check system health
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
@bez What should we do about orders that received the wrong email confirmations?
Cancel or try and reason with customers? Cancelling will cause lost revenue
Confirming now Bob
Metrics are going up
New orders are coming in again
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Can someone place an order and check the confirmation is correct?
Looks like we are back to business
I tested and it worked for me
And the email looks good @shay ?
Well done everyone
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
Thanks @PLAYER and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
@bob see above

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
Please proceed with customer communications on those orders that had wrong confirmations
:star: Know your team and their roles
